programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster navigates the Kennet and Avon Canal from Bath toward London, as his exploration of some of Britain's best waterways and their history continues. Along the way, he proves hopeless at making beer barrels, a little better at stoking steam engines, and a real natural with Freddy the narrowboat horse(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel and Michael have an uncomfortable meeting. Chris catches one of his drivers behaving suspiciously. Joe asks Lisa if she is serious about Mark(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris does not know what to do about Tony Jarvis, the suspected drug smuggler, and warns Kathy to keep her nose out of his business. Mark wants Lisa to meet Annie(n)

programme.desc: Annie is still keeping quiet about her mystery meal. Nick finds it hard to relax without Alice around. Chris sacks Tony Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rick comes to stay with Rachel, Eric and Elizabeth go house-hunting, Melanie finds out about Mark and Lisa's relationship, and Michael makes a fool of himself(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan is determined not to lower the standards of his restaurant. Melanie hears strange noises when she babysits in one of the cabins. Dan makes a fool of himself at the disco(n)

programme.desc: Lynn takes Peter to visit his dad's grave, Melanie insists Joe calls the police about the prowler, and Frank is not impressed with Michael's attitude(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel resents the way the McDonalds have taken over the pub, Andy returns to work a hero, and Mark asks Mike if he can stay the night as he does not want to be near Ken(n)

programme.desc: Mark enjoys being with Mike and Alma, Derek lets Mavis read his dossier on Harry, and Raquel gets fed up with Liz ordering her around(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A spate of petty thefts is traced to a local nursing home, but Blaketon's investigations uncover something far more sinister. Meanwhile, David's first day in his new job goes spectacularly wrong, but Bernie is not surprised. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The beleaguered Cabot Cove residents fall victim to a callous development scheme - all except Jessica, who resolves to get to the bottom of the mystery. Angela Lansbury stars as the amateur sleuth, with William Windom, Ron Masak and Michael Horton(n)

programme.desc: The inspector bans all the garage staff from smoking on duty - with dangerous consequences. Starring Reg Varney, Doris Hare, Anna Karen and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.sub-title: Love Is What You Make It

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper goes into hospital with suspected appendicitis and George looks forward to some peace and quiet - but Jerry shows up on his doorstep with other ideas. Roy Kinnear guest stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ashfordly Hall throws open its doors to the public, but all hell breaks loose when a collection of valuable Constable sketches is stolen. Mike and Tom are sent to question the chief suspect, an 80-year-old art thief, only to find him mugged and bound to a chair - with none of the paintings anywhere in sight. Nostalgic drama, starring Jason Durr and Ryan Early(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The A-level results are out, and Mark does not want to check his. Melanie makes a statement to the police about the prowler, and Joe tries to convince Frank about the model farm(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris and Kathy return from their holiday in Scotland, Lynn prepares for the opening of her new business, and Frank upsets Kim when he lays on a special meal for her(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike discovers Doug has spent the night at the garage, Jack agrees to take Doug in for 50 pounds a week, and Liz puts the idea into Jim's head that they can run a pub together(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is shocked when Jim resigns from his job, Derek fears he went too far in his dossier, Denise is surprised when Hanif chats her up, and Emily helps out at the Kabin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore, Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for Robert's birthday party are under way, but Rose's surprise contribution to the event ruffles feathers above and below stairs. Mary faces dramatic repercussions when a new farming venture is launched on the Downton estate, while Edith receives more unwelcome news. Hugh Bonneville, Lily James, Michelle Dockery and Laura Carmichael star in the costume drama(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The heads of an Ivy League private college tempt fate by inviting Jessica to lecture to their pupils, and it is not long before the sleuth has a murder to investigate. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor and Louisa make plans for their wedding, but the big day runs far from smoothly, as a drunken vicar leaves Martin with a dilemma. programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey

As she tries to deal with the revelation, Janet is left to take charge of the latest case - the gang shooting of a teenager whose friend is a key witness. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins(n)

credits.actor: Mark Jordon, James Carlton, Ted Robbins

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook

He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin

Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs

Having at least 10 puppies by the age of two has taken a physical toll on her, but there's a more worrying discovery in the operating theatre(n)

programme.title: Cilla

The re-emergence of impresario Brian Epstein then propels her career to greater heights, but this is quickly followed by a new low when her first record fails to set the charts alight, despite being written by two local lads called John Lennon and Paul McCartney. Epstein suggests a change of direction, persuading the singer to take a gamble on the ballad Anyone Who Had a Heart, against her future husband's advice. programme.title: WW2: The True Cost of War
programme.desc: A look at the Battle of Britain, examining the limited resources the British military were left with following the retreat from Dunkirk, and the pressure on German forces to conquer England quickly, so attention could be switched to Russia. programme.title: Cricket on 5
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day four of the Second Test at Lord's, where the five-match series continued. Australia tend to have a lot of success against England on this ground, and have only suffered three defeats here in Test matches since 1899 - those being in 1934, 2009 and 2013. The 2013 contest resulted in a 347-run victory for the hosts that was built largely on a scintillating first-innings bowling performance by Graeme Swann, who took 5-44 as the Aussies were dismissed for the paltry sum of 128(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Train
programme.desc: The inside story of the trains used by the monarchy for more than 150 years, from its lavish past as the royal express of excess to the present Queen's mobile 'home from home'. The programme meets staff who have served on the train for many years, from the foreman who accidentally walked in on the Queen in her nightgown, to the mischievous 86-year-old signalman who encouraged Diana, Princesss of Wales to play practical jokes on his friends(n)

programme.title: William & Harry: Princes at War?

programme.desc: Adaptation of the mystery novel, starring Max Irons and Terence Stamp. Charles, the son of the assistant commissioner at Scotland Yard returns to London from Cairo following the end of the Second World War, only to discover his fiancee's grandfather has been murdered. He uses his father's connections to become involved in the investigation, and finds the dead man's second wife a likely suspect. However, nobody in the household has an alibi, and Charles' fiancee may have the clearest motive for murder. With Gillian Anderson and Glenn Close(n)

programme.title: Cricket on 5
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day five of the Second Test at Lord's, where the contest was brought to a conclusion. There hasn't been a drawn Ashes Test match here since 1997, and of the five fixtures to take place since then Australia have come out on top three times, while England have emerged victorious twice. The most famous series to take place during that time span was in 2005, when England reclaimed the urn for the first time since 1987 with a 2-1 series victory, which actually began with Australia taking a 1-0 lead following a victory in the First Test here at the home of cricket(n)

programme.title: Sports Direct: Secrets of the Mega Sports Factory
programme.desc: The story behind the sporting-goods retailer established by Mike Ashley, a divisive figure who overcame retail corruption, workplace scandals and conflict with the City to become a high-street giant. Former staff tell stories from the shop floor, while an undercover reporter reveals what conditions were really like in the infamous Shirebrook warehouse. As Sports Direct has expanded, it has faced unprecedented challenges, and after buying House of Fraser, the programme asks if Ashley can turn that business around(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild

First through Dr Ramshaw's consulting room door is Roy, a sprightly 92-year-old, whose carpal tunnel syndrome keeps him awake at night, while Dr Cordell treats Angela, who has a persistent nosebleed and has started seeing strange lights in her field of vision(n)

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.sub-title: Building Britain's Railways with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks(n)

programme.title: Big Week at the Zoo
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Nick Baker return to Yorkshire Wildlife Park, near Doncaster, to celebrate the work that British zoos perform in the service of worldwide conservation. In the first edition, they drop in on three one-month-old endangered tiger cubs, Yorkshire Vet star Peter Wright befriends a tame tapir. JB Gill assists as a pygmy hippo is given a much-needed dental procedure and helps determine the sex of a baby sloth, while attempts are made to encourage two rare Chinese alligators to breed(n)

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of Refood, which diverts rotting food from landfill and plugs it into the National Grid. If all the food wasted by UK homes was turned into energy, it could power more than 1.2 million homes. There is also a look at Sue and John Wellfare, who have made it their mission to recycle the unrecyclable. The pair sort items by hand, from biscuit wrappers and baby-food sachets to crisp packets, and have set up drop-off points for what would otherwise end up on the ground(n)

programme.title: Manhunt: Catch Me if You Can
programme.desc: The tactical police officers go in search of a convicted murderer who is suspected of committing a serious assault while out on licence from prison, hunting day and night through squats, hostels and homeless haunts. They also mount a surveillance operation on a man thought to be a member of a gang who committed an attempted burglary and go on the trail of a serial shoplifter who failed to turn up at court(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Toughest Prisons
programme.sub-title: Secrets of the World's Toughest Prison
programme.desc: Journalist Paul Connolly experiences life as an inmate in some of the world's most notorious prisons. In this instalment, he serves time with some of Poland's most dangerous criminals in Piotrkow, a maximum security facility where isolation, strict discipline and zero tolerance keep prisoners locked up for 23 hours a day. Paul learns first-hand about the punishment room where violent inmates are subdued, is subject to an intimate body search, watches as the guards search for contraband, and discovers why inmates are forced to eat meals in their cells(n)

programme.title: Coneheads
programme.desc: An alien couple and their daughter crash-land on Earth and try to blend into suburban society, all the while dodging the immigration authorities and their increasingly suspicious neighbours. With no mechanic willing to venture out until the next day, they have to stay overnight and begin to be picked off and dispatched with gruesome violence, realising that their dark secrets have resurrected a local blood-soaked medieval legend. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Artist and dairy farmer Penny Timmis hosts the second dinner party in Shropshire, hoping her dentist posing as a magician is enough to divert her guests' attention away from the possible animal hairs in their food(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: LED lighting salesman Richard Mercer hosts the third dinner party in Shropshire. He prepares a main course of paella, before suggesting a game of 'Guess the Gadget' to entertain his guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: David Jason's Secret Service
programme.desc: The actor examines stories of heroism in the Second World War and explores a world of assassins, saboteurs, commandos, real-life super-villains and the ultimate femmes fatales. David also gets a taste of what it was like to be sent undercover as he takes flight in Britain's last remaining Lysander aircraft - the plane used to get agents in and out of Nazi-occupied Europe(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the A&E department of London's King's College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. Also brought in by helicopter is a father-of-four who fell from scaffolding as he repaired a roof, while other cases include former docker Bill, who has hurt his knee while drinking at his local working men's club(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The stories of children and teenagers brought into A&E. Sixteen-year-old Ellie has suspected spinal injuries after being thrown from her horse and crushed against a fence, while consultant Jacqui Butler treats four-year-old Destiny, who has a fishbone stuck in his tonsils from his lunchtime curry. Plus, a pregnant woman experiences intense pain following a sudden haemorrhage(n)

Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the A&E department of London's King's College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Sicily, where a vast network of tunnels leads to a subterranean salt-processing plant. Kate Quilton looks at free range hens, and is surprised to learn that most chicks on British farms have their beaks trimmed at birth. In the Netherlands, she visits a new cutting-edge farm designed using the latest scientific advances in hen welfare. Plus, in Scotland, Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the origins of haggis, and then faces the ordeal of delivering some unwelcome news to an unsuspecting audience(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a couple in Worcestershire who have given up the rat race, selling their town house to buy some land in the country. With just £50,000 they set out to construct their own home from the most unlikely of starting points - four old portable office buildings. Schoolteacher Lauren, who discovered an enormous pond hidden under decking in the back garden, sets out to transform the plot into a beach-themed holiday retreat. As George's Italian road trip continues, he travels to the old town of Mathi, north of Turin, where he finds a modernist gem of a home(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple looking for a home in Warrington, who do not seem to be able to agree on the best approach to house hunting, which has led to six months with no progress. In Liverpool, Phil Spencer comes to the aid of a successful entrepreneur looking for a house in Crosby, but who has very specific ideas(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help house-hunters find their dream properties in the Peak District, guiding them through the complex world of viewings and agents. Kirstie is with single mother Anne, who sold her home in Sheffield to move to the countryside with her teenage son. However, a short-term stay with her parents has turned into eight months, so Kirstie has her work cut out to find Anne a suitable property fast. Phil is with Andrea and James, who are still searching for their ideal place after two years(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to The Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to thrash out their issues. Next, father and son duo Mick and Chris Emanuele from La Tavola Calda want to put Cheryle and Anthony's sleep issues to bed. Eventually, matters are resolved and the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Savage Kingdom
programme.desc: A small group of wild dogs seek a safe place to raise their six puppies, but faces a constant struggle to protect the offspring from predators(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A builder falls at work and dislocates his shoulder in such an unusual way it takes a five-strong team to pull it into place. Alan, who is in his sixties and had one leg amputated in 2009, comes in with the other dangerously swollen following a holiday in Goa, and 83-year-old James has to be persuaded by senior sister AnnMarie to accept treatment for a cyst on his knee(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: First-hand accounts of deadly wildfires in Paradise, California, that wiped out homes, towns and lives, telling the stories of the emergency responders and those who escaped(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The trials and tribulations of coming of age in Britain, and the challenges faced in dealing with people in the legal and social hinterland between childhood and adulthood. In Warrington, PC Ruth Stott is called to attend a group of teenagers after 999 calls from intimidated local homeowners, while in Crewe, PCs Bryony Hancock and Charlotte Wilson are called to intervene in a case of alleged online bullying(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Great Pyramid: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Documentary shedding light on a fresh discovery at the foot of Egypt's Great Pyramid, which may explain how the ancient Egyptians were capable of building such a vast structure without the aid of computers of complex machinery. At the base of the monument, archaeologists have uncovered a relic of Khufu, the pharaoh whose remains were interred in the Great Pyramid. What they found was a ceremonial boat, laid out in 'flat-pack' form, which may prove to be the key in explaining the crucial role ships and water played in the pyramids' construction(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Yanni's in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter Avgoustiou, a chef so set in his ways that he hasn't allowed any changes to his menu since the Greek restaurant opened in 1984. Peter's family is concerned the business is becoming trapped in the past, and standards are slipping, but he seems determined to stick to his guns(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads off to Israel on the trail of the fresh date, with his search taking him to a vast water purification plant where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is used to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton is in Belfast to meet a sausage-maker whose sales suffered in the wake of the recent World Health Organisation report linking processed meat to cancer, while Matt Tebbutt is in Kent, where experts have perfected the science of growing apples(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp comes to the rescue of Dave and Kate, whose house has been flooded(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: As Jonathan and Michael prepare their chateau for the big wedding, they have a sewage problem that threatens to take the whole first floor out of action(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen put the remaining teams to the test with a secret recipe challenge, then gives them a chance to impress judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin with their showmanship by making a chocolate showpiece inspired by the theme of the four seasons(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

New parents Matt and Jodie Annen are also looking for a family home, but can't agree on where it should be. programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Billy Connolly sets out to fill in the gaps in his own family history. Starting in Glasgow, the city of Billy's birth, he hopes to find out more about his mother's relatives. He believes they hailed from Ireland, but records concerning his great-great-grandmother only deepen the mystery. Billy also heads to India, where he unearths details of the parts two of his ancestors played in historical events(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Four decades ago, Neasden-born model and actress Twiggy, then known as Lesley Hornby, found her life transformed when a reporter spotted her photo on the wall of a London hairdresser, and she was dubbed the 'face of the 60s'. Suddenly the 16-year-old was catapulted into life as a supermodel, travelling the world, mixing with some of its biggest stars and eventually appearing in films such as The Boyfriend and The Blues Brothers. In the 100th episode of the genealogy show, Twiggy examines her family's past, where she discovers a history involving strong women and a hint of crime(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates what Hitler intended to do with the priceless works of art stolen by the Nazis from across Europe, as well as his obsession with religious artefacts(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The wildlife on the great plains of Africa, Asia, North America and the Arctic, which cover a quarter of the land on Earth. The distinctive Tibetan fox is captured on film for the first time, while a herd of caribou migrates 2,000 miles across the tundra in search of grass. Night vision equipment tracks a pride of 30 lions hunting elephants in the tropical African savannah near one of the region's few remaining waterholes. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An insight into woodlands around the world, from the stunted conifers cloaked in snow and ice in the Arctic forests, to the broadleaf woods in North America and Europe. Also featured are the 100m-high giant redwoods of California, as well as Madagascar's unusual upside-down trees, which store water in their swollen trunks and harbour strange wildlife. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes some of the varied species to be found in tropical rainforests, from the amusing courtship rituals of the birds of paradise and a chimpanzee group defending its territory to a flying-frog mating orgy and the sinister parasitic fungi cordyceps, which eat their victims alive(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The wildlife on the great plains of Africa, Asia, North America and the Arctic, which cover a quarter of the land on Earth. The distinctive Tibetan fox is captured on film for the first time, while a herd of caribou migrates 2,000 miles across the tundra in search of grass. Night vision equipment tracks a pride of 30 lions hunting elephants in the tropical African savannah near one of the region's few remaining waterholes. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An insight into woodlands around the world, from the stunted conifers cloaked in snow and ice in the Arctic forests, to the broadleaf woods in North America and Europe. Also featured are the 100m-high giant redwoods of California, as well as Madagascar's unusual upside-down trees, which store water in their swollen trunks and harbour strange wildlife. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes some of the varied species to be found in tropical rainforests, from the amusing courtship rituals of the birds of paradise and a chimpanzee group defending its territory to a flying-frog mating orgy and the sinister parasitic fungi cordyceps, which eat their victims alive(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of guitarists taken from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Includes electric performances by Carlos Santana, Mark Knopfler, Peter Green and the Edge, and acoustic material by John Martyn, John Renbourn, Bert Jansch and Paco Pena(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of 1970s-era axe-men, acoustic virtuosos and thumping riff merchants, in a compilation of guitar-heavy performances from the BBC TV archives. Guitar gods including Jimi Hendrix, Pete Townshend, Peter Green and Johnny Winter are joined by, among others, flamenco maestro Manitas De Plata, bottleneck bluesman Ry Cooder and straight-up rockers AC/DC and Thin Lizzy. Plus, Fleetwood Mac, the Jam and Be Bop Deluxe and Nils Lofgren(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Knights of the Garter arrive at Windsor Castle for their annual lunch with members of the monarchy, which includes a complicated procession through the palace. The royal archives allow cameras to view nearly 200 years of photo albums, including the thousands of photographs from the Queen Mother's collection, and the castle clock-maker faces a race to ensure all 450 timepieces on the estate are turned back(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Duke of Edinburgh, in his capacity as Ranger of the Great Park, takes a tour of Windsor's grounds and points out the modifications he has made during his tenure, including a deer park and a farm shop selling local produce. He then competes in the Royal Horse Show, which also features Prince Harry. Meanwhile, the Prince of Wales and the Duchess of Cornwall host a special banquet for the Prince's Trust(n)

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: How the world's biggest ships manage to get the job done, beginning with the Saipem 7000 as she sails from Stavanger in Norway to the Ula Oilfields(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Billy Connolly sets out to fill in the gaps in his own family history. Starting in Glasgow, the city of Billy's birth, he hopes to find out more about his mother's relatives. He believes they hailed from Ireland, but records concerning his great-great-grandmother only deepen the mystery. Billy also heads to India, where he unearths details of the parts two of his ancestors played in historical events(n)

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Inspector George Gently and The Professionals star Martin Shaw is the latest celebrity to go in search of his roots, and he's particularly interested in finding out more about his paternal grandfather Edwin, who was rarely spoken about by his family. The actor has only one photo of his ancestor, which shows him aged roughly 17 and wearing a military uniform. By zooming in on the picture, Martin's brother was able to identify their grandfather's badge, which suggests he was in the Royal Marines. Now, by meeting with a military historian, Martin tries to fill in the rest of the story, and in the process learns more about Edwin's role in defending Birmingham against German air assaults during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: DJ, actor and presenter Reggie Yates sets out to trace his ancestry. His mother and father were both born in Ghana, but he never knew his grandparents, and he is keen to know where his surname comes from, believing it might be Dutch. However, as he looks further back, his family tree springs some unexpected surprises(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Nazi occupation of Holland, which began relatively peacefully with Dutch civil servants continuing to run their own country, but eventually resulted in many thousands of deaths through the attempted elimination of Jewish citizens and starvation of the non-Jewish inhabitants. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline
programme.desc: Prince Andrew discusses his stint in the Falklands and what it's really like to confront your own mortality, while Prince Edward's decision to quit his marine training is examined(n)

programme.desc: The highs and lows of Prince Harry and Prince William's roles in the forces, featuring an interview with Harry in which he discusses a planned secret deployment in Afghanistan(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Property magnate Paul Whight invites Tony Robinson and the team to investigate his stately home in Essex to learn more about the De Veres, better known as the Earls of Oxford, who once lived there. In an in-depth search of the building and garden, the archaeologists gradually make discoveries that shed light on the history of the home and the aristocratic family(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Kenfig, south Wales, to search for the remains of the once-thriving harbour town believed to have been buried beneath sand dunes in a series of storms more than 500 years ago. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming(n)

The business in run by Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works at a radiotherapy clinic next door, and is determined to introduce hospital-style techniques to the kitchen by sealing everything in plastic bags(n)

programme.title: Born Famous
programme.desc: Spice Girl Mel B's daughter Phoenix Brown travels to Hyde Park in Leeds, to visit the house where her mother grew up and spends time with her aunt and grandmother. Phoenix does a shift for the same pizza chain her mum worked for as a teenager, and finds out more about the community and its large student population(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. The second edition focuses on the decisions Jade Goody made which proved disastrous for her life in the spotlight, including a stint on Celebrity Big Brother which left her reputation and business in ruins after a series of abusive rows with Bollywood actress Shilpa Shetty triggered a huge wave of complaints about racist bullying(n)

programme.title: Kathy Burke's All Woman

programme.desc: The chef heads to floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's in Plainfield, New Jersey, but struggles to get his message across to indomitable owner Shelley. programme.title: The Great Train Robbery: The Hidden Tapes
programme.desc: With access to tapes that have been hidden for over 40 years, this documentary goes behind the legend of the 1963 heist to uncover the true story of the crime of the century. Through tape recordings made by one of the key masterminds, the film explores the truth about the background to the robbery, and the audacious planning to carry it off, as well as revealing how one of the men managed to escape the police and live off his share of the loot until he died a few years ago(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Archaeologists reveal the mysteries of one of Egypt's greatest female pharaohs, Hatshepsut. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart McCracken and Elmore Victor head to Liverpool for what they expect to be a routine repossession, but instead face the most challenging eviction of their careers. Behind the front door, they find a critically ill two-year-old child with major heart problems, who is on constant life support and unable to breathe without a ventilator. Meanwhile, Brian O'Shaughnessy and Del Anglin chase £2,000 worth of debt owed by a business based in Kent, and recover £4,000 from a recycling business in Buckinghamshire. programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Bryan settles into his new home after a long journey from Mexico and one of his first tasks is to learn how to peel fruit. Fellow chimp Joline raises concerns when it is revealed she is suffering from aching joints, hair loss and a lack of appetite. Medical tests prove inconclusive so she is sent for exploratory surgery(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The vets examine 17-year-old chimpanzee Freddie, who is lacking his usual zest for life, and use a fibre-optic camera to look inside his intestines and identify the problem. programme.title: Back from the Dead: The John Darwin Story
programme.desc: The story of John and Anne Darwin, who were sentenced to more than six years' imprisonment for an insurance fraud which saw the husband fake his own death in March 2002 and start a new life in Panama. The scheme was uncovered when John appeared at a police station in 2007 claiming to be a missing person suffering from amnesia(n)

programme.title: War and Peace
programme.desc: Three Russian aristocrats - flighty Natasha, pacifist Pierre and military officer Andrei - endure a series of tumultuous events as Naopleon Bonaparte's armies invade their country. While Andrei is sent to the front line, Pierre is drawn into a loveless marriage, ignorant of Natasha's feelings for him. Historical drama based on Leo Tolstoy's novel, starring Audrey Hepburn, Henry Fonda and John Mills(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles' Other Mistress
programme.desc: Documentary film examining the life of Australian heiress Dale 'Kanga' Tryon, who was Camilla Parker Bowles' rival in the 1970s for the affections of the Prince of Wales. Expelled from the royal circle for speaking to the Press about her relationship with the Windsors, she suffered a breakdown that saw her sectioned under the Mental Health Act, and died in 1997(n)

programme.title: Welcome to the Mosque
programme.desc: Robb Leech provides an insight into what life is like behind a mosque's walls, from rituals and marriage, to racism experienced by older community members when they were growing up. He witnesses segregation and meets younger members of the community, who are afraid of 'free-mixing' with the opposite sex. He also captures the response to the news of the three London schoolgirls who left Britain to join Isis jihadists in Syria, including frantic phone calls and the journey to Istanbul with the girls' families to find them(n)

programme.title: Roman Britain from the Air
programme.desc: The Romans were the first to invade and occupy Britain and their architecture and way of life have left a legacy that has lasted 1,600 years. Christine Bleakley takes to a helicopter to view remains that are best seen from the air, while historian Michael Scott is on the ground to explore further what each sky-high vantage point reveals. Their journey takes them from London, across to Wales and north to Hadrian's Wall, visiting fascinating sites that include the best-preserved Roman legionary barracks anywhere in Europe, the 6,000-seat amphitheatre at Caerleon and town walls that stretch for more than a mile(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
